title: Price of lithium-ion battery cells
subtitle: Representative estimate of the price of [battery cells](#dod:battery-cell) for [lithium-ion batteries](#dod:lithium-ion-battery), across all major cell chemistries. Prices are in US dollars per [kilowatt-hour](#dod:watt-hours), adjusted for inflation.
note: This data is expressed in constant 2024 US$ per kilowatt-hour.
caption:
Price of lithium-ion battery cells

Representative estimate of the price of battery cells for lithium-ion batteries, across all major cell chemistries. Prices are in US dollars per kilowatt-hour, adjusted for inflation.

This line chart tracks the representative estimate of the price of battery cells for lithium-ion batteries from 1991 to 2024, with values expressed in constant 2024 US dollars per kilowatt-hour. The vertical axis uses a logarithmic scale ranging from $100 to $10,000. The data line begins in 1991 near the $10,000 mark. Between 1991 and 2000, the price fluctuates but remains generally above the $2,000 level, dipping slightly around 1993 before rising again toward the mid-1990s. Starting around 2000, the line enters a sustained downward trajectory. By 2005, the price has fallen below the $1,000 threshold. The decline continues through the subsequent decade, crossing the $500 mark around 2011. From 2015 onward, the descent accelerates on the log scale, dropping below $200 around 2017. In the final years shown, from 2020 to 2024, the line sits in the lowest portion of the chart, ending just above the $100 grid line. The visual pattern displays a consistent long-term reduction in cost over the thirty-three-year period, moving from the top of the chart's range to the bottom.
